title: Asking a big favour
setting: You need your friend Lukas to help you move furniture this weekend.

steps.speaker: Lukas
steps.es: Sali! Was git's? Du tönsch, als bruuchsch öppis.
steps.en: Hi! What's up? You sound like you need something.

options.es: Ja, ehrlich gseit han ich e Bitte. Du dörfsch ruhig nei säge.
options.en: Yes, honestly I have a request. You can absolutely say no.
options.feedback: Excellent — naming it as a favour and giving him an out shows real respect.

options.es: Ich bruuch dini Hilf am Samschtig.
options.en: I need your help on Saturday.
options.feedback: Direct, but a softer framing (and an easy out) makes a big ask land better.
'You have to' pressures a friend. Ask, don't command — and leave room to decline.

steps.speaker: Lukas
steps.es: Klar, frag nu. Um was gaht's?
steps.en: Sure, just ask. What's it about?

options.es: Ich zügle am Samschtig. Chönntsch mer mit de Möbel hälfe?
options.en: I'm moving Saturday. Could you help me with the furniture?
options.feedback: Clear, specific, and polite in the conditional. Easy for him to say yes to.

options.es: Es isch chli vil Arbet, aber...
options.en: It's a bit of work, but...
options.feedback: Don't undersell or trail off — state the ask clearly so he can decide.

options.es: Nu chli Möbel träge, nüt Grosses.
options.en: Just carrying a little furniture, nothing big.
options.feedback: Downplaying it can backfire if it turns out to be a lot. Be honest about the scope.

steps.speaker: Lukas
steps.es: Klar mach ich das! Wänn söll ich cho?
steps.en: Of course I'll do that! When should I come?

options.es: Super, merci viilmal! Am nüni? Ich lade dich nochhär zum Ässe ii.
options.en: Great, thank you so much! At nine? I'll treat you to a meal after.
options.feedback: Grateful and reciprocal — offering something back is exactly right after a big ask.

options.es: Am nüni wär super. Danke dir!
options.feedback: Clear and warm. A little thank-you gesture later wouldn't hurt.
Assuming his time is yours undoes the goodwill. Be considerate of his schedule.

steps.speaker: Lukas
steps.es: Abgmacht! Es Ässe nimm ich gärn. Bis Samschtig!
steps.en: Deal! I'll happily take the meal. See you Saturday!

options.es: Du rettsch mich, merci! Bis Samschtig.
options.en: You're saving me, thanks! See you Saturday.
options.feedback: Warm and appreciative — he'll feel genuinely valued. Perfect.

options.es: Super, ich bi dir öppis schuldig.
options.feedback: Acknowledging the favour keeps the friendship balanced. Nice.
A bit flat for someone doing you a big favour. Show more warmth.
